title: Enfield Food Pantry
summary: A food access project that links pantry operations, produce display, modest material systems, and trail infrastructure into a civic threshold for rural public life.

title: Deconstruct Reconfigure
summary: A reuse-driven produce stand that extends the Enfield project through salvaged material, reversible assembly, and small-scale public infrastructure.

title: Borinquen Healing Center
summary: A rehabilitation campus organized around movement, landscape, and care, where architecture supports physical recovery through spatial sequence and environmental comfort.

title: A Chair is a Toy!
summary: A body-scale study of furniture as play, extending the Borinquen project's interest in movement, posture, and recovery.

title: Design District Canteen
summary: A canteen project exploring structure as a framework for gathering, food, and public interior life.

title: Hunters Point Housing
summary: A housing proposal investigating how collective domestic space, environmental systems, and urban edges can be organized into a more generous residential framework.

title: Curanto Cookhouse
summary: A cookhouse project organized around ceremony, ground, fire, and collective preparation.

title: York Prize
summary: A projected competition study extending questions of ritual, ground, and public form from the Curanto Cookhouse.

title: Wood Street Pool
summary: A public natatorium that tests whether cold-climate recreation infrastructure can approach net-zero performance while expanding civic comfort.

title: Woven Pavilion
summary: An unsited material and assembly study exploring weaving, enclosure, and light.

title: Sustainable Education
summary: A school and teacher institute project in Nepal developed through Cornell University Sustainable Design, integrating cultural continuity, drainage, climate resilience, and educational infrastructure.
